title: State introduces new digital literacy curriculum in public schools
text: The state education board on Tuesday approved a new digital literacy curriculum aimed at preparing students for an increasingly technology-driven world. The program will introduce coding basics, online safety practices, and critical thinking skills related to digital media. Officials said the curriculum will be phased in over the next two academic years. Teachers welcomed the update but noted that additional training and resources will be necessary to implement it effectively.

title: University expands scholarship program for low-income students
text: A major public university announced an expansion of its scholarship program targeting low-income students, increasing both the number of awards and the funding available. The initiative aims to reduce financial barriers and improve graduation rates. Administrators said the program will prioritize first-generation college students. Advocacy groups praised the move but emphasized the need for broader systemic reforms to address college affordability.

title: City unveils plan to modernize public transit system
text: City officials revealed a comprehensive plan to modernize the public transit system, including new electric buses, upgraded train stations, and expanded routes. The proposal is part of a broader effort to reduce traffic congestion and emissions. Transit advocates welcomed the investment but raised concerns about potential fare increases. The project is expected to roll out in stages over the next decade.

title: Airline tests new fuel-efficient aircraft on regional routes
text: A major airline has begun testing a new fleet of fuel-efficient aircraft on several regional routes as part of its sustainability strategy. Company representatives said the planes consume significantly less fuel and produce fewer emissions compared to older models. Aviation experts noted that while the move is a step forward, widespread adoption will depend on cost and infrastructure readiness.

title: Researchers discover new method for water purification
text: Scientists have developed a novel technique for purifying water using a combination of advanced filtration and chemical processes. The method has shown promising results in removing contaminants more efficiently than traditional systems. Researchers believe the technology could be particularly useful in areas with limited access to clean water. Further testing is planned before the method can be deployed on a large scale.

title: Space agency announces timeline for next lunar mission
text: A national space agency has released a timeline for its upcoming lunar mission, outlining key milestones leading up to the planned launch. The mission aims to conduct scientific experiments and test new technologies for future exploration. Officials said the program represents a significant step toward long-term human presence beyond Earth. Critics, however, questioned the cost and prioritization of space spending.

title: Retail chain shifts strategy to focus on online sales growth
text: A nationwide retail chain announced a strategic shift toward expanding its online presence, citing changing consumer behavior. The company plans to invest in e-commerce infrastructure and streamline its supply chain. Analysts say the move reflects broader industry trends accelerated by recent economic conditions. Some store closures are expected as part of the transition.

title: Startup secures funding to develop eco-friendly construction materials
text: A construction technology startup has secured significant funding to develop environmentally friendly building materials. The company aims to reduce the carbon footprint of construction projects by using recycled and sustainable resources. Investors expressed confidence in the growing demand for green building solutions. Industry observers say the success of the venture will depend on scalability and cost competitiveness.
